name: Transcribe Audio & Video
description: Transcribe speech from an audio or video file on the server's filesystem. Routes through the configured STT provider (local Faster-Whisper or remote API). Video files are decoded to audio first via ffmpeg.
systemPromptAddition:
## Transcription

When the user asks you to transcribe an audio or video file, call `transcribe_file` with the absolute path to the file. The tool handles common audio formats (wav, mp3, flac, ogg, m4a) and video formats (mp4, mov, mkv, webm) — for video, it extracts the audio track via ffmpeg first.

If the user references a file by name (e.g. "transcribe the meeting recording"), the path may have been passed in a prior turn as a system note (look for `Files referenced:` lines) or in the user's chat as `@video/<name>` / `@audio/<name>` which the server resolves to an absolute path before you see the message. Use those paths verbatim.

Return the transcript to the user in clean prose — strip leading/trailing whitespace, fix obvious time-formatting (7.23 → 7:23 AM if context suggests a time), and offer a short summary when the transcript runs over ~500 words.

function.name: transcribe_file
function.description: Transcribe speech in an audio or video file. Returns the transcript text plus basic metadata (duration, detected language). Common formats supported: wav/mp3/flac/ogg/m4a for audio, mp4/mov/mkv/webm for video.

path.description: Absolute path to the audio or video file on the server's filesystem.

language.description: Optional language hint as a BCP-47 / ISO-639-1 code (e.g. 'en', 'es', 'fr'). Whisper auto-detects when omitted.
